speaker: alice
text: Brilliant environmental news today Alex - for the first time in the Goldman Prize's 35-year history, all six winners are women! These amazing activists from around the globe are taking on oil companies, stopping destructive mining, and protecting biodiversity in their communities.

speaker: charlie
text: What legends! I love hearing about people like Yuvelis Morales Blanco from Colombia who actually stopped commercial fracking by taking on massive oil companies. She grew up in a fishing family along the Magdalena River and decided enough was enough.

speaker: alice
text: And in brilliant species recovery news, Japan's red-crowned crane has officially been removed from their threatened species list! These beautiful birds were feared extinct in the early 1900s, but conservation efforts have grown the population to nearly 2000 birds. Sometimes called the symbol of good fortune in Japan, they're definitely bringing good luck to conservationists worldwide.
